text.title: Gemini API Integration Core Rules
text.summary: Six testable rules for Gemini API integration.

rule.statement: Pick Flash for cost-sensitive paths (≤20% of Pro pricing); Pro only when quality eval requires it. Default = Flash.
rule.rationale: Flash is cost-competitive with Haiku; Pro pricing kicks in only when needed.

rule.statement: For media files >10 MB or any video/audio, use the Files API (upload + reference) instead of inline base64. Track file TTL (48h default).
rule.rationale: Inline base64 inflates the request and blows the request-size limit; Files API also enables caching.

rule.statement: generation_config is always set explicitly (temperature, max_output_tokens, response_mime_type). Default of "leave it to the SDK" produces inconsistent behaviour across versions.
rule.rationale: Explicit config locks behaviour against SDK upgrades.

rule.statement: safety_settings covers all 4 standard categories (HARASSMENT, HATE_SPEECH, SEXUALLY_EXPLICIT, DANGEROUS_CONTENT) with explicit thresholds. Omitting a category accepts the SDK default which may change.
rule.rationale: Without explicit thresholds, BLOCK_REASON appears with no caller fingerprint.

rule.statement: Retry on 429 (rate-limited) with exponential backoff capped at 60s and ≤5 attempts. No retry on 400 (bad request) — surface to caller.
rule.rationale: 429 is transient; 400 is structural and retries waste quota.

rule.statement: When response.candidate.finish_reason == "SAFETY" or "BLOCKLIST" or "OTHER", caller surfaces the block_reason to the application layer; do not silently coerce to empty string.
rule.rationale: Silent BLOCK_REASON is a top source of "AI answered nothing" production bugs.

rule.statement: Preconditions hold → wire Gemini client per the procedure.
Vendor is not Gemini → skip; use the relevant vendor methodology.
